evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars clearly show 4-class proportions across 5 conditions summing to 100%; y-axis percent labels and legend are readable.
evidence.typography: No pdf.fonttype=42 or svg.fonttype setting in gen_figure.py; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (rasterized) DejaVuSans — not editable vector text, and DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default rather than a deliberate font choice.
evidence.palette: 4 harmonious colors used (#4C72B0, #55A868, #C44E52, #CCB974); however, green (#55A868) and red (#C44E52) are paired as adjacent categories, which is problematic for deuteranopia/protanopia colorblind viewers.
evidence.layout_density: Figure fills the panel well (6x4 inches), tight_layout applied, no gaping whitespace, no label overlap, y-range exactly 0–1 as specified.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legend placement (upper right) is reasonable but overlaps the tallest bar segment in some conditions; caption does not include a specific numerical finding that would satisfy a reviewer.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF font is Type 3 (embedded bitmap glyphs via DejaVuSans), not Type 1/TrueType with fonttype=42; text in the PDF is not selectable/editable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial in size.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption correctly describes what the figure shows (stacked bars, conditions, classes) but contains no numerical claim or specific finding — just states values come from data.json.

overall_strengths: Clean, well-structured stacked bar chart that correctly fulfills the brief: 5 conditions, 4 classes, bars normalized to 100% with percent y-axis labels.
All required output files (pdf, png, svg, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) are present and non-trivial; layout is tight with no whitespace waste or label collisions.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 and rcParams svg.fonttype settings, resulting in rasterized Type 3 DejaVu Sans fonts in the PDF — text is not selectable or editable by downstream LaTeX workflows.
Caption lacks a specific numerical finding or claim; it only restates the figure structure and defers to data.json, which Reviewer 2 would likely flag as uninformative.
